title: Dehumidifying Coil Rating Parameters
summary: Standard rating parameters for dehumidifying coils per AHRI Standard 410.

title: Air Face Velocity Range
summary: Typical air face velocity range for dehumidifying coils.

title: Chilled-Water Temperature Range
summary: Entering chilled-water temperature range for coil rating.

title: Water Velocity Range
summary: Water velocity range for coil rating.

title: Ethylene Glycol Solution Parameters
summary: Rating parameters for coils with ethylene glycol solution.
evidence_quote: For ethylene glycol solution: 1 to 6 fps, 0 to 90°F entering dry-bulb temperature, 60 to 80°F entering wet-bulb temperature, 10 to 60% aqueous glycol concentration by weight

title: Sensible Heat Ratio Range
summary: Typical sensible heat ratio range for dehumidifying coils.
evidence_quote: The air-side ratio of sensible to total heat removed by dehumidifying coils varies in practice from about 0.6 to 1.0

title: Comfort Coil Face Velocity
summary: Recommended air face velocity range for comfort dehumidifying coils.
evidence_quote: Dehumidifying coils for comfort applications are frequently selected in the range of 400 to 500 fpm air face velocity.

title: Standard Rating Point for Unitary Equipment
summary: Standard airflow rating point for unitary equipment.
evidence_quote: a standard rating point has been 33.4 cfm per 1000 Btu/h (or 400 cfm per ton of refrigeration effect), not to exceed 37.5 cfm per 100 Btu/h for unitary equipment

title: Refrigerant Superheat at Coil Suction
summary: Refrigerant superheat requirement at coil suction outlet.
evidence_quote: refrigerant vapor superheat at coil suction outlet is 6°F or higher

title: Evaporator Refrigerant Saturation Temperature
summary: Evaporator refrigerant saturation temperature range for coil rating.
evidence_quote: Evaporator refrigerant saturation temperature: 30 to 55°F at coil suction outlet

title: Ceiling Plenum Return Air Temperature Rise
summary: Temperature rise in return air plenum below roof deck during cooling season.
evidence_quote: The air temperature in a return air plenum directly below a roof deck is usually higher by 3 to 5°F during the air-conditioning season than in a ducted return.

title: Sill Grille Deflection Recommendation
summary: Deflection angle recommendation for linear bar grilles in sill applications based on distance from wall.
evidence_quote: If the device is installed more than 12 in. from the vertical surface, a linear bar grille with a fixed 15 to 30° deflection is recommended.

title: Control Valve Location in HTW Systems
summary: Control valves are commonly located in return lines to reduce operating temperature and prevent plug erosion.
evidence_quote: Control valves are commonly located in the return lines from heat transfer units to reduce the valve operating temperature and to prevent plug erosion caused by high-temperature water flashing to steam at lower discharge pressure.

title: HTW Generator Minimum Water Flow
summary: HTW generators require a minimum water flow at all times to prevent tube failure.
evidence_quote: HTW generators require a minimum water flow at all times to prevent tube failure.

title: Closed Hydronic System Definition
summary: Definition of a closed water system with no more than one point of interface with a compressible gas.
evidence_quote: A closed water system is defined as one with no more than one point of interface with a compressible gas or surface, and that will not create system flow by changes in elevation.
